intro: Lucide and LottieFiles diverge first on pricing model: Lucide uses free pricing with a free tier, LottieFiles uses freemium pricing with a free tier. Everything else flows from that commercial choice. Lucide is built for modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set and is not a fit for projects needing multiple visual weights, duotone styles, or mobile-native icon packs. LottieFiles is built for design and product teams integrating lottie animations across web and mobile apps and is not a fit for solo designers needing only a lightweight web editor or teams avoiding subscription tools. On the scoring side, Lucide rates 5/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency; LottieFiles rates 5/5 and 3/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Lucide you accept that single visual weight limits stylistic variation, while LottieFiles comes with the reality that pricing has crept up for team plans. Neither side is free. Lucide's headline strength in the data is that actively maintained fork of feather with 1000+ icons.
quick_take: Lucide is for modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set; LottieFiles is for design and product teams integrating lottie animations across web and mobile apps; decide based.
verdict_a: Choose Lucide if you are modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set and a free pricing model fits how you buy.
verdict_b: Choose LottieFiles if you are design and product teams integrating lottie animations across web and mobile apps and a freemium pricing model fits how you buy.

faq.a: Lucide uses a free model, while LottieFiles uses a freemium model, and both publish a free tier. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Lucide to LottieFiles?
faq.a: Lucide has low lock-in (oss icon library); LottieFiles has low lock-in (lottie json open format). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Lucide; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.
Both Lucide and LottieFiles score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so DX isn't the tiebreaker here. Decide on fit, pricing, or lock-in instead.

faq.q: Is LottieFiles a good alternative to Lucide?
faq.a: LottieFiles is built for design and product teams integrating lottie animations across web and mobile apps and explicitly not for solo designers needing only a lightweight web editor or teams avoiding subscription tools, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Lucide for modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
